grant_and_term.initial_term: 20 years from 1st day of month Hotel opens
grant_and_term.successor_term: 10 years on then-current terms (may be materially different)
grant_and_term.aop_term: Typically ends 3 years after Hotel opening

fee_obligations.capex_reserve: 4% of GRR deposited monthly to franchisee-controlled account

site_selection_and_construction.plans_approval: Must submit design development documents for approval; cannot begin construction without approval
site_selection_and_construction.extension_available: Yes, with $5,000 fee, at franchisor option
site_selection_and_construction.fb_operations_plan: Must be approved; any material changes require prior written approval

training_obligations.core_management_training: All Core Management must complete brand standard training
training_obligations.gm_vacancy_deadline: 6 months; interim GM must complete GMU
training_obligations.replacement_training: Replacement personnel must complete training within 90 days
training_obligations.failure_to_complete: Must immediately hire substitute and arrange training
training_obligations.conventions_mandatory: Owner conventions may be required; fees $2,000-$5,000/attendee

termination_triggers_non_curable: Cease operating Hotel or identifying with Proprietary Marks
Contest ownership of or try to register Hyatt IP
Actions reflecting adversely on Hyatt or Proprietary Marks
Violation of law including trade restriction laws
Repeated defaults (3+ defaults in 12 months even if cured)

transfer_restrictions.approval_required: All transfers of FA, Hotel/assets, or Controlling Ownership Interest
conditions: Transferee signs then-current FA (may be materially different) or assumes existing FA
Transferee agrees to renovate Hotel and FF&E
Franchisee and guarantors sign release (if state law allows)
transfer_restrictions.death_disability_transfer: 6 months (12 months if heir cannot meet conditions)

noncompete.during_term: No explicit covenant; cannot be Competing Brand Owner
noncompete.competing_brand_owner_definition: Entity owning/managing brand with 5+ competing hotels worldwide

guaranty.who_must_sign: Owners of Controlling Ownership Interest as specified by franchisor
guaranty.guarantor_monetary_threshold: Set per deal in Exhibit B-1; increases annually by CPI
guaranty.annual_reporting: Financial statements or documents certifying threshold compliance

liquidated_damages.formula: Greater of $4,000 × rooms or (lesser of 36 months or remaining months) × Average Monthly Revenue × 8%
liquidated_damages.brand_damages_concept: Damages to brand from early termination; acknowledged as real and meaningful
liquidated_damages.de_identification_penalty: $5,000/day for failure to de-identify after termination

dispute_resolution.venue: Within 10 miles of Hyatt's principal business address (currently Chicago, IL)
dispute_resolution.governing_law: Illinois (subject to FAA and state overrides)
dispute_resolution.class_action_waiver: Not explicitly stated but standard in arbitration

indemnification.scope: Application, development, operation (including negligent/intentional conduct), breach
indemnification.defense_obligation: Franchisee must defend Hyatt at franchisee's expense
indemnification.proportionate_share: Must pay Hotel's share of expenses defending claims involving Hotel and other Hyatt Network Hotels

force_majeure.casualty_provisions: Must repair if cost ≤ Damage Threshold (60% market value or insurance proceeds); may terminate if cost > Damage Threshold with Termination Agreement
force_majeure.closure_provisions: FA term extends during closure; no royalty/SSC during closure unless insurance proceeds received
force_majeure.reconstruction_deadline: Commence within 4 months, reopen within 24 months of closure

distinctive_clauses.description: All Guest Information is franchisor's property; franchisor has unlimited access to PMS and computer systems
distinctive_clauses.impact: Franchisee does not own guest data generated at its own hotel during operation

distinctive_clauses.description: All inventions and innovations relating to Brand Hotel are franchisor's sole property (work made-for-hire)
distinctive_clauses.impact: Franchisee has no IP rights in anything created for the hotel

distinctive_clauses.description: In 14 specified states, no statement or acknowledgement can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance
distinctive_clauses.impact: Protects franchisees in registration states from anti-reliance provisions

distinctive_clauses.description: 3+ defaults in 12 months constitute non-curable default even if each was individually cured
distinctive_clauses.impact: Repeated operational issues can lead to termination even if cured each time

distinctive_clauses.description: Franchisor may modify System Standards, Hotel System, and System Services at any time; franchisee must comply
distinctive_clauses.impact: Open-ended obligation to invest additional capital to comply with changing standards
